You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 24.12 (commit: eeec5405).

## Issue to Resolve

[Reader IA] Bring back the Discover Onboarding for new users
When a new user entered the Reader for the first time we would show the `Discover` feed with a "Welcome" message and a button leading to a "Choose your topics" screen, which essentially followed those tags and made Discover be populated with stuff based on those tags.

All the code for that is still present in the app and is still shown if the user unsubscribes from ALL their tags (when the user has 0 subscribed tags), here's an example:

https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/assets/5091503/7b77b04a-fa3d-4e6a-852c-95ac587b97cd

This (0 tags subscribed) used to be the state of a new account as well, but recently the `dailyprompt` tag is being subscribed to by default on new accounts, which causes the Welcome screen to be skipped and the Reader Discover feed to be populated, which makes it hard for the user to configure their tags and see interesting stuff in that feed for the first time accessing the Reader in the app.

We want to bring back this simple Onboarding Flow to provide a better user experience, especially on the first time using the Reader.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/ReaderDiscoverViewModel.kt`
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/interests/ReaderInterestsViewModel.kt`
- `WordPress/src/main/res/layout/reader_interests_fragment_layout.xml`
- `WordPress/src/main/res/values/strings.xml`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 72→            when (val result = readerTagRepository.getUserTags()) {
 73→                is ReaderRepositoryCommunication.SuccessWithData<*> -> {
 74→                    userTagsFetchedSuccessfully = true

115→                    val distinctTags = ReaderTagList().apply { addAll(tags.distinctBy { it.tagSlug }) }

182→    private fun shouldShowDiscoverOnboarding(userTags: ReaderTagList): Boolean {
183→        // Keep existing behavior for a truly empty tag list.

185→

187→        // user completes Discover onboarding.
188→        val hasNonDefaultTags = userTags.any { !it.tagSlug.equals(BLOGGING_PROMPTS_TAG_SLUG, ignoreCase = true) }
189→        if (hasNonDefaultTags) return false
190→
191→        return !appPrefsWrapper.isReaderDiscoverOnboardingCompleted()
192→    }

196→        _uiState.addSource(readerPostCardActionsHandler.followStatusUpdated) { data ->

205→                var hasChangedBlogs = false
206→
207→                for (j in mutableBlogs.indices) {
208→                    val blog = mutableBlogs[j]

210→                        mutableBlogs[j] = blog.copy(isFollowed = data.following, isFollowEnabled = data.isChangeFinal)
211→                        hasChangedBlogs = true

content.text:
Implemented a minimal, targeted fix to restore the **Discover onboarding** for new Reader users even when the `dailyprompt` tag is auto-followed.

### What changed

- **Discover onboarding logic (ReaderDiscoverViewModel)**
  - Treats a user as having “no meaningful tags” when their followed tags are **empty** OR **only `dailyprompt`** *and* they have **not completed** Discover onboarding.
  - Shows the existing Welcome/“Get Started” empty state in that case.

- **Interests screen entry logic (ReaderInterestsViewModel)**
  - Previously, opening “Choose your topics” would immediately close if *any* tag existed (including `dailyprompt`).
  - Now it allows the interests flow to load when the user’s only followed tag is `dailyprompt` and onboarding isn’t completed.

- **Persisted completion flag**
  - Added a deletable pref key `READER_DISCOVER_ONBOARDING_COMPLETED` (cleared on logout).
  - Marked as completed when saving interests succeeds.

### Files updated

- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/ReaderDiscoverViewModel.kt`
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/interests/ReaderInterestsViewModel.kt`
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/prefs/AppPrefs.java`
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/prefs/AppPrefsWrapper.kt`

 53→    fun start(
 54→        currentLanguage: String,
 55→        parentViewModel: ReaderViewModel?,
 56→        entryPoint: EntryPoint
 57→    ) {
 58→        if (isStarted && this.currentLanguage == currentLanguage) {
 59→            return
 60→        }
 61→        isStarted = true
 62→        this.currentLanguage = currentLanguage
 63→        this.parentViewModel = parentViewModel
 64→        this.entryPoint = entryPoint

 69→    private fun loadUserTags() {
 70→        updateUiState(InitialLoadingUiState)
 71→        viewModelScope.launch {
 72→            when (val result = readerTagRepository.getUserTags()) {
 73→                is ReaderRepositoryCommunication.SuccessWithData<*> -> {
 74→                    userTagsFetchedSuccessfully = true
 75→                    userTags = result.data as ReaderTagList
 76→                    when (entryPoint) {
 77→                        EntryPoint.DISCOVER -> checkAndLoadInterests(userTags)
 78→                        EntryPoint.SETTINGS -> loadInterests(userTags)
 79→                    }
 80→                }
 81→                is ReaderRepositoryCommunication.Error -> {
 82→                    if (result is ReaderRepositoryCommunication.Error.NetworkUnavailable) {
 83→                        updateUiState(ConnectionErrorUiState)
 84→                    } else if (result is ReaderRepositoryCommunication.Error.RemoteRequestFailure) {
 85→                        updateUiState(RequestFailedErrorUiState)
 86→                    }
 87→                }
 88→                ReaderRepositoryCommunication.Started -> Unit // Do nothing
 89→                ReaderRepositoryCommunication.Success -> Unit // Do nothing
 90→            }
 91→        }
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    private fun checkAndLoadInterests(userTags: ReaderTagList) {
 95→        val shouldLoad = userTags.isEmpty() || (
 96→            userTags.all { it.tagSlug.equals(BLOGGING_PROMPTS_TAG_SLUG, ignoreCase = true) } &&
 97→                !appPrefsWrapper.isReaderDiscoverOnboardingCompleted()
 98→            )
 99→
100→        if (shouldLoad) {
101→            loadInterests(userTags)
102→        } else {
103→            parentViewModel?.onCloseReaderInterests()
104→        }
105→    }

107→    private fun loadInterests(userTags: ReaderTagList) {
108→        updateUiState(InitialLoadingUiState)
109→        viewModelScope.launch {
110→            val newUiState: UiState? = when (val result = readerTagRepository.getInterests()) {
111→                is ReaderRepositoryCommunication.SuccessWithData<*> -> {
112→                    readerTracker.track(AnalyticsTracker.Stat.SELECT_INTERESTS_SHOWN)
113→
114→                    val tags = (result.data as ReaderTagList).filter { checkAndExcludeTag(userTags, it) }
115→                    val distinctTags = ReaderTagList().apply { addAll(tags.distinctBy { it.tagSlug }) }
116→                    when (entryPoint) {
117→                        EntryPoint.DISCOVER -> ContentUiState(
118→                            interestsUiState = transformToInterestsUiState(distinctTags),
119→                            interests = distinctTags,
120→                            doneButtonUiState = DoneButtonDisabledUiState(),
121→                            titleVisible = true
122→                        )
123→                        EntryPoint.SETTINGS -> ContentUiState(
124→                            interestsUiState = transformToInterestsUiState(distinctTags),
125→                            interests = distinctTags,
126→                            doneButtonUiState = DoneButtonDisabledUiState(R.string.reader_btn_done),
127→                            titleVisible = false
128→                        )
129→                    }
130→                }
131→                is ReaderRepositoryCommunication.Error.NetworkUnavailable -> {
132→                    ConnectionErrorUiState
133→                }
134→                is ReaderRepositoryCommunication.Error.RemoteRequestFailure -> {
135→                    RequestFailedErrorUiState
136→                }
137→                else -> {
138→                    null
139→                }
140→            }

152→    private fun observeDiscoverFeed() {
153→        // listen to changes to the discover feed
154→        _uiState.addSource(readerDiscoverDataProvider.discoverFeed) { posts ->
155→            launch {
156→                val userTags = getFollowedTagsUseCase.get()
157→                if (shouldShowDiscoverOnboarding(userTags)) {
158→                    _uiState.value = DiscoverUiState.EmptyUiState.ShowNoFollowedTagsUiState {
159→                        parentViewModel.onShowReaderInterests()
160→                    }
161→                } else {
162→                    if (posts != null && posts.cards.isNotEmpty()) {
163→                        _uiState.value = DiscoverUiState.ContentUiState(
164→                            convertCardsToUiStates(posts),
165→                            reloadProgressVisibility = false,
166→                            loadMoreProgressVisibility = false,
167→                        )
168→                        if (swipeToRefreshTriggered) {
169→                            _scrollToTopEvent.postValue(Event(Unit))
170→                            swipeToRefreshTriggered = false
171→                        }
172→                    } else {
173→                        _uiState.value = DiscoverUiState.EmptyUiState.ShowNoPostsUiState {
174→                            _navigationEvents.value = Event(ShowReaderSubs)
175→                        }
176→                    }
177→                }
178→            }
179→        }
180→    }

182→    private fun shouldShowDiscoverOnboarding(userTags: ReaderTagList): Boolean {
183→        // Keep existing behavior for a truly empty tag list.
184→        if (userTags.isEmpty()) return true
185→
186→        // New accounts are auto-subscribed to Blogging Prompts ("dailyprompt"). Treat that as "no tags" until the
187→        // user completes Discover onboarding.
188→        val hasNonDefaultTags = userTags.any { !it.tagSlug.equals(BLOGGING_PROMPTS_TAG_SLUG, ignoreCase = true) }
189→        if (hasNonDefaultTags) return false
190→
191→        return !appPrefsWrapper.isReaderDiscoverOnboardingCompleted()
192→    }
193→
194→    private fun observeFollowStatus() {
195→        // listen to changes on follow status for updating the reader recommended blogs state immediately
196→        _uiState.addSource(readerPostCardActionsHandler.followStatusUpdated) { data ->
197→            val currentUiState: DiscoverUiState.ContentUiState = _uiState.value as? DiscoverUiState.ContentUiState
198→                ?: return@addSource
199→            val mutableCards = currentUiState.cards.toMutableList()
200→            var hasChangedCards = false
201→
202→            for (i in mutableCards.indices) {
203→                val card = mutableCards[i] as? ReaderCardUiState.ReaderRecommendedBlogsCardUiState ?: continue
204→                val mutableBlogs = card.blogs.toMutableList()
205→                var hasChangedBlogs = false
206→
207→                for (j in mutableBlogs.indices) {
208→                    val blog = mutableBlogs[j]
209→                    if (blog.blogId == data.blogId && blog.feedId == data.feedId) {
210→                        mutableBlogs[j] = blog.copy(isFollowed = data.following, isFollowEnabled = data.isChangeFinal)
211→                        hasChangedBlogs = true
212→                    }
213→                }
214→
215→                if (hasChangedBlogs) {
216→                    mutableCards[i] = card.copy(blogs = mutableBlogs)
217→                    hasChangedCards = true
218→                }
219→            }
